goal: Land G3 (Knowledge) parallel-query orchestrator with entity-keyed temporal-priority merge in a new ucil-daemon::g3 module, exercised by the frozen test executor::test_g3_parallel_merge that asserts parallelism, newest-wins-on-conflict, highest-confidence-on-agreement, different-entities-union, per-source-error/timeout resilience, and master-deadline trip.
plan_summary: Mirroring WO-0047's G1 shape (orchestrator + DI-seam trait + dual-deadline ladder + frozen test) and WO-0068's cross-group lessons (per-source deadline as unconditional const, NOT min'd with master_deadline). New file crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs holds G3Source trait (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam), G3Query, G3FactObservation, G3SourceOutput, G3Outcome, G3MergedFact, G3MergeOutcome, plus pub async fn execute_g3 (parallel orchestrator under master+per-source deadlines) and pub fn merge_g3_by_entity (entity-keyed temporal-priority merger per master-plan §5.3). The frozen test test_g3_parallel_merge lives at module root in crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs (DEC-0007) — NOT inside mod tests {} — supplied with three local TestG3Source impls (DEC-0008 §4) returning canned (entity, fact, confidence, ts) tuples; SA1..SA8 cover parallelism, temporal-priority, confidence-tiebreak, entity-union, partial-error, partial-timeout, master-deadline, empty-source. Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract names file/line/restore for verifier. No new deps; async-trait already a workspace dep (WO-0068). No mocks of MCP/JSON-RPC/subprocess — real subprocess wiring (CodebaseMemoryG3Source/Mem0G3Source impls calling the F05/F06 plugins) is DEFERRED to a follow-up production-wiring WO. classify_query/parse_reason re-export deferral from WO-0067/0068 stays deferred (will land in the daemon-side classify-then-dispatch pipeline WO).
scope_in: 1. NEW file crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs (DEC-0005 module-coherence; one cohesive feat commit even if 600-800 LOC). Public surface: G3Query (entities: Vec<String>, max_results: usize, min_confidence: f64), G3FactObservation { source_id: String, entity: String, fact: String, confidence: f64 in [0.0, 1.0], observed_ts_ns: u128, validity_window: Option<(u128, u128)> }, G3SourceOutput { source_id: String, status: G3SourceStatus (Available/TimedOut/Errored), elapsed_ms: u64, observations: Vec<G3FactObservation>, error: Option<String> }, G3Outcome { results: Vec<G3SourceOutput>, wall_elapsed_ms: u64, master_timed_out: bool }, G3MergedFact { entity: String, winning_fact: String, winning_source_id: String, winning_confidence: f64, winning_ts_ns: u128, conflict_count: u32, agreement_count: u32 }, G3MergeOutcome { merged: Vec<G3MergedFact>, total_observations_in: usize, total_entities_out: usize }, pub trait G3Source: Send + Sync (fn source_id(&self) -> &str; async fn execute(&self, query: &G3Query) -> G3SourceOutput).

2. Constants in g3.rs: pub const G3_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000); pub const G3_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500). Per WO-0068 lessons-learned 'For executor' #2: the per-source deadline is an UNCONDITIONAL const, NOT min'd with master_deadline. The per-source only wins on true global stalls (sleeper > 4.5 s); the master only wins on tight budgets (master < 4.5 s). Document the inversion inline at the deadline-computation site.

3. pub async fn execute_g3(query: G3Query, sources: Vec<Box<dyn G3Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>, master_deadline: Duration) -> G3Outcome — fans out via tokio::join_set or futures::future::join_all wrapped in tokio::time::timeout(master_deadline, ...). Per-source futures wrapped in a private run_g3_source helper that applies tokio::time::timeout(G3_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, source.execute(...)) and on timeout returns G3SourceOutput { status: TimedOut, ... }. Decorate with #[tracing::instrument(skip(sources), fields(source_count = %sources.len()))] per master-plan §15.2 (this is async/IO orchestration — tracing applies, unlike the WO-0067 pure-deterministic ceqp.rs).

4. pub fn merge_g3_by_entity(outputs: &[G3SourceOutput]) -> G3MergeOutcome — entity-keyed temporal-priority merge per master-plan §5.3 lines 473-477. Algorithm: (a) collect all observations from non-Errored / non-TimedOut sources (Errored/TimedOut sources contribute zero observations to the merge); (b) group by `entity` field; (c) within each entity group, partition by `fact` text equality: same-fact observations form an 'agreement cluster' (winner: highest confidence; ties: newest observed_ts_ns; ties: lexicographic source_id), conflicting-fact observations form a 'conflict set' (winner: newest observed_ts_ns; ties: highest confidence; ties: lexicographic source_id); (d) emit one G3MergedFact per entity with conflict_count = number of distinct fact strings - 1, agreement_count = number of observations supporting the winning fact. NOT RRF — this is entity-keyed temporal merge, distinct from the rank-based G2/cross-group fusion. Graphiti temporal validity (validity_window) is NOT yet load-bearing (Graphiti = F10, not yet installed); a sentinel comment in the merge body documents the future hook for §5.3 line 475's 'Graphiti temporal validity wins'.

5. Add `pub mod g3;` to crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs alphabetically BETWEEN `pub mod g2_search;` (line 158) and `pub mod lancedb_indexer;` (line 159). Per WO-0068 lessons-learned 'For planner' #4: the alphabetical convention wins — `g3` slots before `lancedb_indexer`. The line range will become 158-160 after insertion.

6. Add a #[rustfmt::skip] pub use block in crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs for the new types, alphabetically positioned BETWEEN the existing `pub use g2_search::...` block (line 187) and `pub use lancedb_indexer::...` block (line 189). Re-export: G3Source, G3Query, G3FactObservation, G3SourceOutput, G3SourceStatus, G3Outcome, G3MergedFact, G3MergeOutcome, execute_g3, merge_g3_by_entity, G3_MASTER_DEADLINE, G3_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE.

7. NEW frozen test crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs::test_g3_parallel_merge — append after the existing test_g1_parallel_execution / test_g1_fusion section. Place at MODULE ROOT inside the executor.rs file (NOT under `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007. Signature: #[cfg(test)] #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::missing_panics_doc)] #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)] pub async fn test_g3_parallel_merge() {}. The substring selector `executor::test_g3_parallel_merge` must resolve cleanly (NOT `executor::tests::test_g3_parallel_merge`). Imports: use crate::g3::{...}; — types come from the daemon crate.

8. Inside test_g3_parallel_merge: define enum TestBehaviour { ReturnObservations(Vec<(String, String, f64, u128)>), Sleep(Duration, Vec<(String, String, f64, u128)>), Error(String), LongSleep(Duration), Empty }. Define struct TestG3Source { id: String, behaviour: TestBehaviour }. Implement #[async_trait::async_trait] G3Source for TestG3Source: source_id returns &self.id, execute matches on behaviour and returns appropriate G3SourceOutput. Per DEC-0008 §4 these are UCIL-internal trait impls (NOT mocks of any external wire format) — the same shape as WO-0047's TestG1Source.

9. Frozen-test sub-assertions (target ≥7 SAs, each carrying `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ...; right: ...` panic body per DEC-0007). SA1 — Parallelism: 3 sources each Sleep(200 ms, ...) must return wall_elapsed_ms in [180, 700) (lower-bound proves at least one source's sleep elapsed; upper-bound proves serial 3×200 ms=600 ms PLUS noise did not happen → parallelism confirmed); dual-bound discipline per WO-0043 lessons. SA2 — Newest-wins-on-conflict: two sources return same `entity = "login_handler"` but different facts ("uses bcrypt" at ts=100 vs "uses argon2" at ts=200); merge winning_fact == "uses argon2" AND winning_ts_ns == 200 AND conflict_count == 1. SA3 — Highest-confidence-on-agreement: two sources return same entity + same fact ("file is in src/auth.rs") with confidences 0.7 (ts=100) and 0.9 (ts=50); merge winning_confidence == 0.9 AND agreement_count == 2 AND conflict_count == 0 (proves confidence beats temporal when facts agree). SA4 — Different-entities-union: source A returns entity="foo", source B returns entity="bar"; merge.merged contains BOTH foo and bar (length 2). SA5 — Partial-Errored: 1 of 3 sources returns Errored; outcome.results carries exactly 1 Errored + 2 Available, AND merge proceeds successfully without including the errored source's contributions. SA6 — Partial-TimedOut: 1 of 3 sources LongSleep(6 s) (> G3_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE = 4.5 s); outcome.results carries exactly 1 TimedOut + 2 Available, wall_elapsed_ms < 5500 ms (per-source 4.5 s ceiling fires before master 5 s). SA7 — Master-deadline-trip: master_deadline=100 ms, all 3 sources Sleep(7 s, ...) → outcome.master_timed_out == true AND wall_elapsed_ms < 2000 ms (master deadline trips before per-source). Per WO-0068 lessons-learned 'For planner' #3: this SA is COMPATIBLE with the unconditional-const per-source deadline — the per-source CONST never gets shortened to master_deadline. SA8 (optional) — Empty-source: zero G3SourceOutput observations → merge_g3_by_entity returns G3MergeOutcome { merged: vec![], total_observations_in: 0, total_entities_out: 0 }.

10. Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract (verifier applies in-place via Edit, NOT git stash; takes md5sum snapshot first; runs targeted cargo test selector; observes SA-tagged panic; restores via git checkout -- <file>; confirms md5sum match). M1 — Sequential execution: in crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs, replace the parallel join (`tokio::join_all` / `JoinSet`) with a sequential `for source in sources.into_iter() { let r = source.execute(&query).await; ... }` loop. Targeted selector: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g3_parallel_merge`. Expected: SA1 (Parallelism) panics with `(SA1) parallel wall < 700 ms; left: 600+, right: 700`. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/g3.rs`. M2 — Inverted temporal comparison: in merge_g3_by_entity's conflict-resolution branch, replace `if obs.observed_ts_ns > current_winner.observed_ts_ns` with `if obs.observed_ts_ns < current_winner.observed_ts_ns` (or `>` flipped to `<`). Targeted selector: same as M1. Expected: SA2 (Newest-wins) panics with `(SA2) newest fact wins on conflict; left: "uses bcrypt", right: "uses argon2"`. M3 — Inverted confidence comparison: in merge_g3_by_entity's agreement-cluster branch, replace `if obs.confidence > current_winner.confidence` with `if obs.confidence < current_winner.confidence`. Expected: SA3 (Highest-confidence) panics with `(SA3) highest confidence wins on agreement; left: 0.7, right: 0.9`. The verifier applies each mutation in isolation (one at a time), confirms targeted SA fails, restores file, runs full test to confirm restoration, then proceeds to next mutation.

11. NEW file scripts/verify/P3-W9-F07.sh (chmod +x). Bash strict mode (set -euo pipefail). Exits 0 iff `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g3_parallel_merge --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/p3-w9-f07-test.log` exits 0 AND grep -qE '^test executor::test_g3_parallel_merge \.\.\. ok$' /tmp/p3-w9-f07-test.log. Echoes `[PASS] P3-W9-F07: G3 parallel-merge frozen test green` on success, `[FAIL] P3-W9-F07: ...` on failure. Mirrors scripts/verify/P3-W9-F03.sh / P3-W9-F04.sh shape from WO-0068. NO mocks. NO env-gated short-circuits — the test is fully synthetic (TestG3Source local impls; no subprocess, no API key).

11. The Memory Curator agent narrative-synthesis layer (master-plan §5.3 line 478-479) — that lives in the agent layer (Phase 3.5), not in F07. F07's merge output is the structured G3MergeOutcome; agent-narrative enrichment is downstream.

13. Production-tier confidence aggregation (e.g. Bayesian fusion across same-fact agreements). F07 uses simple max-confidence; future WOs may revisit if §5.3's 'highest-confidence version' semantics need richer aggregation.

lessons_applied: WO-0067/0068/0069 #1: pre-bake M1/M2/M3 mutation contract in scope_in (file/line/restore) for verifier in-place application
WO-0068 #2: frozen-test at MODULE ROOT in executor.rs, NOT under `mod tests { ... }` — substring selector `executor::test_g3_parallel_merge` requires module-root placement
WO-0068 #3: per-source deadline is an UNCONDITIONAL const, NOT min'd with master_deadline — avoids the WO-0068 contradiction where SA-master-trip and per-group-cap were mutually exclusive
WO-0067 #4: master-plan §15.2 tracing spans APPLY to F07 (async/IO orchestration), unlike pure-deterministic ceqp.rs where they did NOT apply
WO-0068 #5: alphabetical placement wins for `pub mod g3;` (between g2_search and lancedb_indexer) and `pub use g3::{...};` block
WO-0067/0068/0069 #6: DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic bodies `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ...; right: ...` for trivial mutation diagnosis

WO-0069 #7: word-ban grep covers PRODUCTION-side files only (g3.rs, verify script); test impls named TestG3Source / *G3Source under #[cfg(test)] in executor.rs are EXEMPT (precedent: WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0068 lessons-learned 'For executor' #5)
WO-0068 #8: NO MOCKS of MCP/JSON-RPC/subprocess — UCIL-owned DI seam (G3Source trait, DEC-0008 §4); real subprocess wiring of CodebaseMemoryG3Source / Mem0G3Source DEFERRED to a production-wiring WO
WO-0067/0068/0069 #9: standing coverage workaround now 26 WOs deep — env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov for substantive measurement; coverage-gate.sh failure carved out per AC23 standing protocol
WO-0068/0069 #10: AC36/AC37 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake — verifier may re-run from clean session; three pre-existing flake escalations are standing scope_out items
WO-0067/0068/0069 #11: gate-side artefact carve-outs in forbidden_paths — verification-reports/** and escalations/** are NOT forbidden
WO-0069 #12: when scope_in prescribes contradictory rules (e.g. WO-0068 #18 positional vs alphabetical), pick ONE and document — this WO picks alphabetical for `pub mod g3;` placement
